books.title: Drowning in Demands: How to Stop Freezing and Speak Up with Confidence
books.subtitle: Practical Tools for Managers Who Avoid Under Pressure
books.desc: You have more on your plate than fits in your hours. stop panicking about money and think clearly.
books.long_desc:
You have more on your plate than fits in your hours.

This book is for mid-level manager running a cross-functional teams who the numbers spiral and I can't make it stop. It doesn't promise to make the feeling stop. It names what's happening in language you can use the next time it happens.

Chapter by chapter, you'll work with how input accumulates past capacity and what happens in your body when it does. Not through exposure or worksheets or morning routines — through recognition, descent, and integration. The practice the book asks for can happen on a subway, between meetings, or at 3am.

You'll walk away knowing what your body is doing and why it makes sense. Not fixed. Not healed in any final sense. But no longer alone with the pattern.
